narrative_design_spec.synopsis: In the haunted forest village of Hollowmere, young witch Nessa Vale returns home after years away to find the community gripped by dread, superstition, and a string of uncanny disturbances. Her estranged childhood friend, Silas Thorn, is being blamed for the hauntings, but the truth is tangled in old rituals, half-buried memories, and a village secret everyone has helped keep in different ways. As Nessa investigates with wary villagers, a calculating headwoman, a compromised shrine keeper, and a hard-bitten hunter, she must decide whether Silas is an innocent scapegoat, a damaged witness, or something more dangerous. The tone is eerie, intimate, and morally ambiguous, with no romance and a strong emphasis on personal history, communal guilt, and dread.

character_data.name: Nessa Vale
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: young woman witch, pale freckled skin, amber eyes, dark auburn hair in a loose braid with stray strands, sharp thoughtful features, black wool cloak over a layered moss-green dress, bone and rowan charm necklace, herb satchel, ink-stained fingers, wary and resolute expression
dialogue_examples: If the forest wanted you dead, it wouldn't bother with whispers.
I remember enough to know when this village is lying.
Fear makes simple stories. Magic never does.
I'm not here to absolve anyone. I'm here to find what was buried.

character_data.name: Silas Thorn
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: young man in his twenties, gaunt face, hollow dark eyes, tangled black hair falling over his brow, old scar across the chin, rough linen shirt, patched dark coat, hands stained with soil and ash, expression caught between defiance and exhaustion
dialogue_examples: They're safer calling me cursed than admitting they helped make this.
Sometimes I hear the well before I reach the trees.
I didn't start the voices, Nessa. I only stopped pretending I couldn't hear them.
If something is wearing my name, I need to know why.

character_data.name: Maelin Briar
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: older woman village head, broad-shouldered, deeply lined face, iron-gray hair braided tightly, stern dark eyes, heavy wool dress layered with a practical shawl, belt hung with keys and iron tokens, severe mouth, commanding posture
dialogue_examples: A headwoman doesn't get the luxury of clean hands.
Superstition keeps children alive when truth would send them wandering into the dark.
You left, Nessa. We stayed and paid the price of staying.
Mercy is easy for those who won't bury the bodies.

character_data.name: Oren Ashdown
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: middle-aged shrine keeper, thin build, deep-set hazel eyes, close-cropped graying hair, weathered black devotional robes sewn with thorn patterns, ash-smudged fingertips, bead cords wrapped around one wrist, calm but haunted expression
dialogue_examples: A ward is only a promise with better handwriting.
The chapel remembers what the villagers edit out.
Sin ripens strangely in a place like this.
I keep records, child. I never claimed I kept innocence.

character_data.name: Tova Reed
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: young adult woman hunter, tawny brown skin, muscular build, cropped auburn hair, sharp nose, practical leather jerkin over rough wool layers, hunting knife at the belt, old bite scar on one hand, guarded stare
dialogue_examples: I don't care whether the dead are offended. I care whether they come through my door.
Silas was always good at walking where he shouldn't.
If there's truth in the trees, it bites.
Choose quickly. Panic spreads faster than fog.

location_data.name: Hollowmere Green
location_data.location_image_prompt: cramped forest village square with leaning timber cottages, mossy roofs, muddy footpaths, hanging iron charms and rowan bundles, a dead ash tree at the center wrapped in warding ribbons, shuttered windows, crude protective sigils carved into doors, oppressive dark fantasy atmosphere

location_data.name: Vale Cottage
location_data.location_image_prompt: interior of an abandoned hedge-witch cottage with low timber beams, stone hearth, shelves crowded with dried herbs and dusty jars, roots hanging from rafters, cracked worktable covered in old charms and candle wax, worn floorboards, signs of old magic and long neglect

location_data.name: Chapel of Briars
location_data.location_image_prompt: small ancient village chapel built of dark stone and warped wood, thorn-carved altar, melted candles, faded saint icons, tangled roots breaking through the floor, walls covered in chalk wards and protective prayers, iron bell rope, decayed sacred atmosphere

location_data.name: The Drowned Well
location_data.location_image_prompt: deep forest clearing ringed by black-barked trees, collapsed stone well half-sunken in moss and stagnant water, standing stones etched with weathered runes, tangled roots, thick ferns, patches of pale fungus, sense of forgotten ritual and lurking dread

scene_data.narrtive_summary: Returning after years away, Nessa arrives in Hollowmere during a public warding rite meant to calm escalating hauntings. Villagers knot ribbons around the dead ash tree and openly name Silas Thorn, her estranged childhood friend, as the source of the unrest. Maelin struggles to contain panic, Tova treats Nessa's return as an inconvenience at best, and Nessa immediately senses the village is afraid of more than ghosts.

Nessa reopens her family's abandoned cottage and discovers its old protections have been disturbed from the inside, not by some outside intruder. Tova arrives with a warning not to interfere in village matters, revealing how fear has turned suspicion into cruelty. Before the scene ends, Silas leaves a covert sign tied to their shared childhood, pushing Nessa toward the memory of the drowned well in Blackbark Forest.

At the decaying Chapel of Briars, Oren Ashdown shows Nessa failing wards and hidden records describing a nameless child lost to the forest years earlier, followed by a secret rite the village refuses to discuss openly. Oren implies the village bound something rather than laid it to rest, and that Silas's family was made to carry blame for the aftermath. Nessa begins to suspect the hauntings are rooted in communal guilt and corrupted old magic rather than a single cursed man.

Another night of disturbances drives Hollowmere toward mob justice, and Nessa confronts Maelin in the village square before fear turns lethal. Maelin admits the village has long lived behind a lie: years ago, a restless dead presence was bound at the forest edge, and several families, including Silas's, were expected to bear the stain to keep order. Tova argues for a practical solution no matter who it harms, sharpening the conflict between survival, guilt, and truth.

Nessa meets Silas in secret at the Drowned Well deep in Blackbark Forest. Silas admits he has been drawn there by voices and has tampered with the ward stones in hopes of recovering the truth of what happened when they were children. He seems lucid one moment and eerily aligned with the haunting the next, leaving Nessa uncertain whether he is witness, victim, accomplice, or vessel.

Back at the chapel, Nessa, Oren, and Tova piece together the full shape of the old sin: the village used witchcraft to contain a death it helped cause, then buried the memory inside ritual and blame. Oren proposes renewing the ward to preserve what little order remains, while Nessa argues that concealment itself is feeding the hauntings. Tova reveals her own family helped enforce the silence, making the final choice feel personal for everyone involved.

At the Drowned Well on the hauntings' peak, Nessa forces Maelin, Oren, Tova, and Silas to confront the truth they each tried to shape or suppress. The bound presence rises through memory, illusion, and a voice that seems to speak through Silas without ever neatly becoming him. Nessa must decide whether to break the ward and let the village face raw truth, or reshape the old magic with full knowledge of its cruelty; either choice leaves Silas's innocence unresolved in troubling ways.

By dawn, Hollowmere is quieter but not cleansed. The dead ash tree stands altered, the bells are silent, and the villagers must live with whatever truth Nessa exposed or bound again. Silas survives changed and uncertain, Maelin and Tova face the consequences of fear-driven choices, Oren remains as keeper of a history that can no longer stay hidden, and Nessa leaves or stays knowing that some hauntings persist because the living learn how to feed them.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: Returning after years away, Nessa arrives in Hollowmere during a public warding rite meant to calm escalating hauntings. Villagers knot ribbons around the dead ash tree and openly name Silas Thorn, her estranged childhood friend, as the source of the unrest. Maelin struggles to contain panic, Tova treats Nessa's return as an inconvenience at best, and Nessa immediately senses the village is afraid of more than ghosts.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Is Silas truly the source of Hollowmere's disturbances, or is the village naming him to contain a darker truth?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Nessa wants to understand what has changed in Hollowmere and why Silas is being blamed before fear hardens into violence.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: Nessa's homecoming shifts from wary observation to personal investigation as she realizes Silas is being used as the village's public answer to a deeper, unspoken fear.

beats.purpose: Establish Nessa's return, the altered mood of Hollowmere, and the desperate public warding rite.
beats.summary: Nessa enters Hollowmere Green to find the villagers gathered around the dead ash tree, knotting ribbons and iron charms in a communal warding rite. Home feels immediately wrong: familiar landmarks remain, but everything is tightened by dread and watchfulness.
beats.revelation: Hollowmere is frightened enough to perform protective magic in public rather than behind closed doors.
beats.player_goal: Take stock of the village and understand why a public rite is underway.
beats.exit_state: Nessa sees at once that Hollowmere is in active crisis, not merely living under old superstition.

beats.purpose: Introduce Silas as the village's chosen scapegoat and show Maelin trying to keep collective fear controlled.
beats.summary: As the rite continues, frightened voices stop speaking in generalities and begin naming Silas Thorn as the source of the unrest. Maelin tries to keep the ritual orderly, but her failure to shut the accusation down reveals how accepted the blame has become.
beats.revelation: Silas is being blamed openly and ritually, not just whispered about in private.
beats.player_goal: Read the crowd and understand why Silas has become the target.
beats.exit_state: Silas's name becomes inseparable from the village's attempt to feel safe.

beats.purpose: Make Nessa's return public and give the player an early stance toward the accusation against Silas.
beats.summary: Someone recognizes Nessa, and the crowd's attention shifts from the ash tree to the witch who left. Maelin greets her with controlled frost, forcing Nessa to respond to Silas's name in full view of the villagers.
beats.revelation: Nessa's history with both Hollowmere and Silas is known enough that her reaction matters to the crowd.
beats.player_goal: Navigate Maelin's public challenge without surrendering leverage.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Nessa respond when Silas is named in front of the crowd?
branch_outcomes: She openly questions the blame on Silas, drawing sharper suspicion from villagers and Maelin.
She asks what happened before taking a side, signaling caution and investigative intent.
She says little and studies the crowd, preserving distance but seeming colder or more complicit.
beats.exit_state: Nessa is no longer a returning stranger at the edge of the square; she is now personally implicated in the village's fear.

beats.purpose: Introduce Tova's practical hostility and show that fear in Hollowmere has become organized, routine, and dangerous.
beats.summary: Tova steps in from the edge of the rite and treats Nessa's return as poor timing rather than relief. Her clipped warning makes clear that the village is already stretched thin by watches, broken nerves, and whatever has been moving at night.
beats.revelation: The disturbances are severe enough that Hollowmere is relying on armed vigilance, not just ritual reassurance.
beats.player_goal: Gauge whether Tova is warning, threatening, or testing her.
beats.exit_state: Nessa understands that the village's fear has become daily practice, not a single night of panic.

beats.purpose: Give the unrest a palpable jolt and bring Silas physically into the scene without resolving his role.
beats.summary: The warding rite stutters when the ribbons on the dead ash tree pull tight in windless air and the iron charms begin to ring. In the surge of fear, Nessa catches a brief glimpse of Silas at the tree line—watching, flinching, and gone before anyone can seize him.
beats.revelation: Silas is close to the disturbances, but his brief appearance leaves open whether he is witness, target, or participant.
beats.player_goal: Discern whether the disruption is truly tied to Silas.
beats.exit_state: Silas stops being an abstract accusation and becomes a living, evasive presence on the edge of the scene.

beats.purpose: Land the scene's thematic turn by showing that secrecy and communal guilt are bound up with the haunting.
beats.summary: Maelin disperses the crowd before fear can turn into a hunt, while Tova warns Nessa not to start pulling at things the village has learned to leave alone. Watching how carefully everyone avoids certain details, Nessa realizes Hollowmere is protecting a lie as much as it is defending itself from the dead.
beats.revelation: The village fears exposure and memory as much as it fears any haunting.
beats.player_goal: Read what the village's silence is really telling her and decide where to begin digging.
beats.exit_state: Nessa leaves the square resolved to reopen Vale Cottage and investigate why Silas's name is being used to cover something older.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: Nessa reopens her family's abandoned cottage and discovers its old protections have been disturbed from the inside, not by some outside intruder. Tova arrives with a warning not to interfere in village matters, revealing how fear has turned suspicion into cruelty. Before the scene ends, Silas leaves a covert sign tied to their shared childhood, pushing Nessa toward the memory of the drowned well in Blackbark Forest.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: What does the inward-broken magic at Vale Cottage mean, and who is using Nessa's family home to warn her or draw her deeper into Hollowmere's secret?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Reopen Vale Cottage as a usable refuge and determine what the disturbed protections reveal before village pressure shuts Nessa out.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: What begins as Nessa reclaiming a safe base becomes proof that someone with intimate knowledge has already breached it, and ends with a secret sign from Silas redirecting her toward the Drowned Well.

beats.purpose: Reestablish Nessa's emotional connection to her family home and frame the cottage as a hoped-for sanctuary.
beats.summary: Nessa forces open the long-shut cottage, letting in gray light and stale air thick with old herbs, dust, and memory. The neglect stings, but the familiar layout steadies her enough to start reclaiming the place as a base of operations.
beats.player_goal: Open the cottage and see whether it can still shelter her.
beats.exit_state: The cottage is open again, and Nessa notices that some of its disorder feels too pointed to be simple decay.

beats.purpose: Introduce the core mystery through physical and magical evidence.
beats.summary: Nessa examines the threshold sigils, hearth ash-circle, and hanging rowan charms, testing where the old protections failed. The break patterns all angle outward, telling her the warding was disturbed from inside the cottage rather than forced by something outside.
beats.revelation: The cottage's protections were broken from within.
beats.player_goal: Figure out whether the cottage was invaded or intentionally opened.
beats.choice_prompt: Which protection does Nessa inspect first?
branch_outcomes: The threshold confirms the seal was parted from the cottage side.
The hearth shows the ash-circle was pushed outward by someone already inside.
A window charm reveals patient, knowledgeable tampering rather than brute force.
beats.exit_state: Nessa rules out a simple external intrusion and starts searching for an inside hand.

beats.purpose: Deepen the mystery by showing the disturbance was deliberate and informed, not random vandalism.
beats.summary: At the worktable and charm shelf, Nessa finds drawers searched but valuables untouched, along with a family knot unpicked cleanly instead of hacked apart. Whoever handled the cottage either knew Vale wardcraft or wanted her to believe they did.
beats.revelation: The breach was deliberate and informed, suggesting message or familiarity rather than theft.
beats.player_goal: Determine whether the disturbance was sabotage, search, or signal.
beats.exit_state: Nessa realizes the cottage has been touched by someone with unsettling knowledge of her family's protections.

beats.purpose: Bring the village's scrutiny into Nessa's private space and escalate the social threat.
beats.summary: Bootsteps outside break the silence, and Tova appears in the doorway with a hunter's wariness and no pretense of warmth. She says she came to see whether Nessa intends to stay, but the visit reads immediately as a warning disguised as courtesy.
beats.revelation: Nessa's return is already being watched and measured by the village.
beats.player_goal: Read Tova's intent and keep control of the encounter.
beats.exit_state: The cottage no longer feels like private ground; Hollowmere's suspicion has followed Nessa to the door.

beats.purpose: Expose how fear has curdled into communal cruelty and let the player define Nessa's stance toward Tova.
beats.summary: Tova tells Nessa not to interfere in village matters, especially not where Silas Thorn is concerned, arguing that frightened people need order more than difficult truths. However Nessa answers, Tova makes clear that suspicion has become a practical tool in Hollowmere: if fear needs a target, the village will give it one.
beats.revelation: Hollowmere is coping by enforcing blame and intimidation, with Silas positioned as the easiest scapegoat.
beats.player_goal: Decide whether to defy, placate, or press Tova for more.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Nessa answer Tova's warning?
branch_outcomes: Defy Tova outright and make clear Nessa will investigate anyway.
Stay measured, keeping Tova talking long enough to expose more of the village's panic.
Invoke Silas directly, forcing Tova to show how personal and ugly the blame has become.
beats.exit_state: Tova leaves with a final warning, and Nessa understands the village's fear is already producing cruelty, not just caution.

beats.purpose: Pivot from public hostility to the private mystery of Silas's covert contact.
beats.summary: In the quiet after Tova goes, Nessa notices a small sign tucked beneath the latch: a childhood knot she and Silas used when marking forbidden places without saying their names aloud. Its doubled loop and downward pull point unmistakably toward the Drowned Well.
beats.revelation: Silas has been close enough to leave Nessa a private message, and he wants her thinking about the Drowned Well in Blackbark Forest.
beats.player_goal: Confirm whether the sign is truly Silas's and what it means.
beats.exit_state: Nessa gains a secret lead that bypasses the village's public story.

beats.purpose: End the scene with a clear investigative direction and a shifted understanding of the cottage.
beats.summary: Nessa pockets the sign and accepts that reopening the cottage has not won her safety, only proof that the past is already active around her. She resolves to pursue what the Drowned Well means, even if that means seeking old records and pushing deeper into truths Hollowmere would rather leave buried.
beats.player_goal: Choose the next line of inquiry opened by Silas's sign.
beats.exit_state: The cottage is reclaimed only in name, and Nessa leaves the scene with the Drowned Well established as her next crucial lead.

color_scheme.reasoning: I chose a muted moss-and-bark palette with warm parchment text to match Hollowmere’s eerie forest-folklore atmosphere. The accent and selection colors echo rowan wood, old ritual tokens, and weathered bronze, while the hover states stay slightly lighter for clear usability without breaking the somber tone. Dialogue and menu text are kept near-ivory for strong readability against dark, decayed backgrounds, and the disabled/muted colors are subdued green-gray tones that fit the setting while remaining distinct from active UI states.
